title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: You are going to read a magazine article about an intellectual process known as critical thinking. For questions 31 – 36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text. Mark your answers on the separate answer sheet.

content.text: Critical thinking is often described as the ability to reflect on and analyze information objectively. However, many experts argue that (0) reaching a truly unbiased perspective is nearly impossible. (9) is frequently neglected is the role that subconscious emotions play in our decision-making. We like to believe we are rational beings, yet we often find ourselves (10) a loss when asked to justify our instinctive reactions. || (11) it not for our inherent cognitive biases, we might process data with clinical precision. (12) only do these biases cloud our judgment, but they also reinforce existing prejudices. (13) the fact that education systems are increasingly focusing on logic, the 'echo chamber' effect of the internet continues to grow. (14) is the power of social confirmation that many individuals prioritize being right over being accurate. (15) provided that we remain aware of these mental shortcuts can we hope to mitigate their effects. Ultimately, there is (16) doubt that the journey toward objective thought is a lifelong endeavor.

questions.explanation: Nominal relative pronoun serving as the subject of the sentence.
Part of the fixed idiomatic expression 'at a loss'.
Inverted second conditional structure (Were it not for...).
Negative inversion used with 'Not only... but also'.
A preposition indicating contrast, followed by a noun phrase.
Used for emphasis in the structure 'Such is/was the [noun] that...'.
Used to introduce a restricted condition, often triggering inversion.
Fixed expression 'there is no doubt that' to express certainty.
